summary: Capture two GetShoppingResults fixtures (thin route SEA-KTI, dense route SEA-BKK) and lock in parseOffersResponse behavior with regression tests.
reason: An audit triggered by an apparent under-reporting bug for SEA-KTI (returns 2 itineraries when web search suggested many carriers fly the route) confirmed the parser is correct: a recursive deep walk of the SEA-KTI response finds the same 2 rows the parser surfaces, and the dense SEA-BKK response parses all 100 itineraries. The original bug premise was Google-side sparsity for the new KTI airport code (Asian carriers have not yet fully published codeshare data under it), not a parser shortfall.
validated_outcome: go test ./internal/gflights/... -run TestParseOffersResponse -count=1; fixtures refresh via `go test -tags capture ./internal/gflights/...`.
